data.title: M&A News: Netflix (NFLX) Strikes $82.7B Deal for WBD Studios, But Will Regulators Approve?
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) has entered a deal to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD) film and television studios in a cash-and-stock transaction valued at about $82.7 billion. The acquisition is set to close in 12 to 18 months, following WBD's planned spinoff of some of its linear TV networks. However, whether the transaction will go through remains uncertain as it is likely to face strict scrutiny from regulators due to concerns about market concentration.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The deal has an equity value of $72 billion and a total enterprise value of $82.7 billion, which includes debt. According to the terms, WBD shareholders will receive $23.25 in cash and about $4.50 in Netflix stock per share.

Under the deal, Netflix will gain control of Warner Bros. film and TV studios, the HBO and HBO Max streaming service, and iconic intellectual property, including the Harry Potter franchise, the DC Universe, Game of Thrones, and Friends.

Ahead of the deal completion, WBD's global networks division, including cable channels such as CNN, TNT Sports, and Discovery, will be spun off into a separate publicly traded company called "Discovery Global."

NFLX Expects Huge Cost Savings

Netflix projects at least $2 to $3 billion in annual cost savings by the third year after the deal closes, primarily through synergies and "optimizing its plans". Also, the transaction is expected to be accretive to GAAP earnings per share by the second year.

Further, Netflix co-CEOs Ted Sarandos and Greg Peters stated the merger will provide "more choice and greater value for consumers" by combining libraries. Further, it will allow the company to expand its U.S. production capacity and original content investment.

The company argues that combining Netflix's global reach with Warner Bros.' renowned franchises and extensive library will offer more opportunities for the creative community to work with beloved intellectual property.

Will the Deal Go Through?

With Netflix already the dominant global streamer, adding HBO Max's 128 million subscribers to its base of over 300 million could trigger antitrust concerns. The deal is expected to face significant scrutiny from U.S. and European regulators due to concerns about market concentration and potential impacts on competition.

U.S. lawmakers, including Senators Elizabeth Warren and Bernie Sanders, have already signaled skepticism, warning the Justice Department to scrutinize the merger closely.

"This deal looks like an anti-monopoly nightmare," Warren said, warning it would create a streaming giant controlling nearly half the market, driving up prices, limiting choices, and putting workers at risk.

Is Netflix Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for Netflix stock is Strong Buy, based on 28 Buy, seven Hold, and one Sell ratings over the past three months. The average NFLX stock price target of $139.13 reflects a potential 38.29% upside.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Bank of America (BAC) Says Stocks to Rally as Markets Price in a January Interest Rate Cut
data.description:
Bank of America (BAC) remains bullish on stocks, saying equities are likely to rally into 2026 as markets price in a January interest rate cut from the U.S. Federal Reserve.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Analyst Aditya Bhave says that markets could soon shift their expectations for a January rate cut despite efforts by central bank officials to sound cautious on monetary policy. In Bank of America's new U.S. Economic Weekly report, Bhave writes, "We wouldn't be surprised if markets start pricing a Jan cut more aggressively in the near term."

Currently, futures traders are placing the odds that the Fed delivers a 25 basis point rate cut at the conclusion of its Dec. 10 policy meeting at 87%. However, the odds that the U.S. central bank also lowers interest rates in January are currently at 27%. Bank of America says that expectations for January could soon change as we near the end of the year.

Convincing the Market

Despite striking a cautious tone on the U.S. economy and timing of future interest rate cuts, Bhave doubts that Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell can convincingly push back against market expectations for further monetary policy easing.

"Given how much data will be released before the Jan meeting, we are skeptical that Powell can be credibly hawkish at the presser," writes the analyst. Bhave adds that Powell could try to emphasize that additional cuts would require "further weakening in the labor data," or argue that a 3.5% to 3.75% policy rate "isn't restrictive in real terms." But markets are likely to stay focused on economic data.

The current Federal Funds Rate is 3.75% to 4.00%.

Is BAC Stock a Buy?

Bank of America's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 18 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 17 Buy and one Hold recommendation issued in the last three months. The average BAC price target of $58.94 implies 8.89% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Apple Issues Huge State-Backed Hacking Warning to Global Users
data.description:
Shares in tech giant Apple (AAPL) fell today as it issued one of its biggest-ever cyber security warnings to its users around the world.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Global Cyber Warning

As part of one of its regular updates to users, Apple sent a new round of cyber threat notifications to clients in 84 countries.

These warnings are issued when tech companies determine that they may have been targeted by state-backed hacking groups.

According to Reuters, Apple's statement, which said the warnings were issued on December 2, carried ​few further details about the alleged ‌surveillance and did not address questions about the number of users targeted or say who was believed to be carrying out the surveillance.

Industry experts said hallmarks of a state-backed campaign include high sophistication, narrow targeting, and the use of methods not commonly deployed by cybercriminal groups.

State Threats Are Rising

The lack of information is also said not to be surprising, as public disclosures could tip off hackers about its detection capabilities or risk exposing users.

Sometimes the exposure can be self-afflicted. Back in August this year, Apple moved quickly to patch a zero-click vulnerability that could have allowed attackers to compromise iPhones, iPads, and Macs without the user even clicking a link. The flaw, which was tied to the way Apple devices process images, left a dangerous opening for attackers to steal sensitive data, including cryptocurrency wallets.

According to the latest State of Information Security Report from IO, 88% of cybersecurity and information security leaders surveyed at UK and US organisations now express concern about state-sponsored cyberattacks.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Canada's Economy Surprises With 54,000 New Jobs in November
data.description:
Canada's economy surprised by adding a better-than-expected 54,000 new jobs in November.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The consensus view of economists surveyed by Reuters (TRI) was for Canada's economy to lose jobs during the month. Instead, November marked the third consecutive month of job gains across the country. The positive labor report has leading Canadian stocks such as Shopify (SHOP) and Lululemon Athletica (LULU) moving higher on Dec. 5.

The strong job gains pushed Canada's unemployment rate down to 6.5% in November from 6.9% in October. Statistics Canada, which released the data, said there were 26,000 fewer people in the labor market last month, helping to push the jobless rate down. In all, Canada's economy added 181,000 new jobs between September and November of this year.

Adjusting to Tariffs

Throughout the spring and summer, Canada's labor market had weakened as the country dealt with the shock of U.S. tariffs and the uncertainty they caused. Economists are now saying that Canada appears to be adjusting to the new realities of life next door to the Trump Administration.

Most of November's job gains were concentrated in part-time work, according to Statistics Canada. The government agency also noted that youth aged 15 to 24 drove most of the employment gains, accounting for 50,000 of the new jobs in November.

Is SHOP Stock a Buy?

The stock of Shopify has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 29 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 17 Buy and 12 Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average SHOP price target of $179.54 implies 10.25% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Lumen Technologies Stock (LUMN) Shifts on an Executive Change
data.description:
Lumen Technologies (LUMN) stock was on the move Friday after the company revealed a change to its executive team. Jim Fowler is taking over as the Chief Technology & Product Officer at Lumen Technologies starting on January 5, 2026. This will have him replace Dave Ward, who is leaving the role to become the President and Chief Architect at Salesforce (CRM).

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

When Fowler takes on his new role at Lumen Technologies, he will report directly to CEO Kate Johnson. His duties will include management of the company's global technology and product strategy. Fowler will be aided by Ward at first, who will stick around until January 23, 2026, to ensure a smooth transition. Investors will also note that Fowler isn't new to Lumen Technologies, having served on its board of directors since 2023. He left his board position in relation to his new role at the company.

Lumen Technologies made sure to note that its technology roadmap won't change with the departure of Ward. Johnson reiterated this when she stated, "Dave's visionary leadership has laid a foundation that will influence Lumen's trajectory for years ahead, and we thank him for his service." Ward's work experience includes two decades at General Electric, which has since split into GE Aerospace (GE) and GE Vernova (GEV).

Lumen Technologies Stock Movement Today

Lumen Technologies stock was down earlier on Friday morning, but has recovered since then and is now up slightly. The stock has also rallied 65.35% year-to-date and 25.43% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Dow Jones Rallies on Tame PCE Inflation Data
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher after the Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) announced that September's Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index increased by 0.3% month-over-month and 2.8% year-over-year, with both readings in line with the consensus estimate. The index was delayed due to the government shutdown, which ended on November 12.

data.title: Nvidia Has a New China Chip Problem! U.S. Senators Launch a New Bill to Ban Exports
data.description:
A bipartisan group of U.S. senators has introduced a new bill to restrict Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and other chip makers from exporting advanced chips to China. The move comes as the Trump administration weighs the possibility of allowing Nvidia to sell its H200 artificial intelligence (AI) chips to China.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The Secure and Feasible Exports Chips Act (SAFE Chips Act) would require the Commerce Department to deny export licenses for advanced chips to China, Russia, Iran, and North Korea for a period of 30 months. After 2.5 years, the department would be required to brief Congress about any proposed rule changes at least one month before they take effect.

China hawks in Washington worry that Beijing could deploy those advanced chips to turbocharge its military with AI-powered weapons and enhanced intelligence and surveillance capabilities.

Security Issues vs. Trade Deal

The SAFE Chips Act, co-sponsored by Republican Dave McCormick and Democrats Jeanne Shaheen and Andy Kim, is a rare bipartisan effort to stop further easing of tech export rules on China, led in part by members of Trump's party. These lawmakers worry that Trump is ignoring potential national security threats to preserve the trade deal with Chinese President Xi Jinping in October.

Pete Ricketts, the Republican chair of the Senate foreign relations East Asia subcommittee, who co-sponsored the bill with Chris Coons, said the U.S. leads in the AI race largely due to its dominance of global compute power. He argued that denying Beijing access to advanced chips is essential. Coons added that the rest of the 21st century will hinge on who wins the AI race and whether AI is built on American values or those of the Chinese Communist Party.

Nvidia Faces Fresh Export Challenges

The SAFE Chips Act presents a new hurdle to Nvidia's push to persuade the Trump administration and Congress to ease export controls that currently bar it from selling its leading AI chips in China. It arrives one day after the world's most valuable company scored a lobbying win by lawmakers shelving a separate export-control bill known as the GAIN AI Act.

Despite recent progress toward export approval for Nvidia's H200 chips, CEO Jensen Huang stated that he was unsure whether China would accept buying the H200 or any degraded chips. Nvidia's earlier attempts to sell its lower-compute H20 chips to China were rejected by Beijing. The country has instructed companies to rely on its own home-grown chips for AI development. Beijing is also trying to reduce reliance on Nvidia's latest chips, with domestic companies Huawei, Alibaba (BABA), and Baidu (BIDU) already working on developing advanced AI chips.

Is Nvidia Stock a Buy?

Despite the ongoing export issues, analysts remain highly optimistic about Nvidia's long-term outlook. On TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $258.10 implies 40.8% upside potential from current levels. Year-to-date, NVDA shares have surged 36.6%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: New York Times Stock (NYT) Flat as It Drags Perplexity AI to Court
data.description:
American newspaper giant The New York Times (NYT) has finally run out of patience with AI artificial intelligence startup Perplexity AI.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

On Friday, the media company dragged Perplexity AI, which is backed by AI chip leader Nvidia (NVDA), to court over claims the AI firm illegally used millions of its articles in training the startup's AI models. The lawsuit comes over a year after The New York Times sent a court letter to Perplexity, asking it to desist from the practice of using its content for its AI models.

Publishers Flag Illegal Content Use by AI Startups

Like rival OpenAI — which has also been sued by the New York Times — Perplexity AI has been a target of lawsuits from multiple publishers accusing the startup of using their content in AI model training without permission. For instance, Perplexity AI is one of four entities sued by social media platform Reddit (RDDT) in October. Reddit claimed these entities scraped its data via Google (GOOGL) search results without permission.

Earlier in June, the UK's national broadcaster BBC also threatened to sue Perplexity AI over similar claims, joining business and technology outlet Forbes and Vogue magazine publisher Condé Nast, both of which raised similar claims last year.

What Has PerplexityAI Said?

Responding to the New York Times last year, Perplexity AI argued that it only engages in "indexing web pages and surfacing factual content as citations to inform responses when a user asks a question." The startup further contended that "the law recognizes that no one organization owns the copyright over facts."

However, Perplexity AI in June entered a strategic deal with Gannett (GCI) to license content from USA Today and over 200 publications across the U.S. that are part of the USA Today Network.

New York Times Continues to Grow Digital Subscriber Base

The New York Times's lawsuit comes as the media giant — which is one of a few that has managed to make a business turnaround amid dwindling ad revenue for traditional publishers — continues to expand its digital subscriber base.

In June, Amazon (AMZN) agreed to pay the newspaper company about $20 to $25 million annually to license a broad range of its content to train its AI models and feed its digital assistant Alexa.

Can You Invest in the New York Times?

On Wall Street, The New York Times' shares currently hold a Moderate Buy consensus rating. This is based on four Buys and two Holds assigned by analysts over the past three months.

At $66.83, the average NYT price target implies approximately 4% upside from the current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Money-Market Funds Surge to Record-High $8 Trillion
data.description:
High yields have led to U.S. money-market funds securing another all-time high of $8 trillion after surpassing $7 trillion in March. Investors have plowed $848 billion into the funds year-to-date, according to Crane Data.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

That comes ahead of the December 9-10 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting, where the Fed is widely expected to cut rates by 25 bps.

"We expect inflows to gradually moderate as rates gradually decline, but historically, yields above 2% should continue to draw inflows into money funds," said TD Securities head of U.S. interest rate strategy Gennadiy Goldberg.

High Yields and Low Risk Keep Money-Market Funds in Demand

Money-market funds have gained popularity in recent years because they generally offer higher yields than bank savings accounts. They're also viewed as a safer bet than equities because they're usually invested in short-term, low-risk assets, like Treasury bills and municipal notes.

Retail portfolio allocation to money-market funds stands between 15% and 20% and is roughly in line with the historical average, according to JPMorgan head of U.S. short-rate strategy Teresa Ho. Despite the potential for rate cuts, Ho expects money-market assets to continue rising next year.

data.title: SoFi Stock Plunges 8% as $1.5 Billion Share Sale Spurs Massive Dilution Fears
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI) stock plunged more than 7% after the digital financial services platform announced a massive offering of $1.5 billion in new common shares. The announcement sent shares trading below $28, wiping out the day's gains, despite the stock being up a massive 92% this year.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The San Francisco-based fintech stated in a regulatory filing that it plans to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, including increasing its capital position, improving capital management, and funding new growth opportunities. However, the sheer size of the offering immediately triggered investor fears over dilution, where issuing a large amount of new stock reduces the value of existing shares.

$1.5 Billion Offering Tanks SOFI Ahead of Critical S&P 500 Decision

The announcement came at a critical time for SoFi. The company was being considered as a potential addition to the S&P 500 (SPX) benchmark index during its quarterly rebalancing this month. Analysts believed SoFi's stock was a prime candidate based on its growth and changes in its shares outstanding. The new index additions are set to be announced on Friday afternoon.

The massive $1.5 billion share sale, however, injects significant uncertainty into the immediate stock performance and capitalization picture. Goldman Sachs (GS) is leading the underwriting for the sale, along with BofA Securities (BAC), Citigroup Global Markets (C), Deutsche Bank Securities (DB), and Mizuho Securities (MFG).

SoFi has seen spectacular performance over the past year, with its stock rising 89% over the last 12 months. The decision to raise such a large amount of cash via equity, despite the high trading price, suggests an aggressive move to capitalize on its high valuation and prepare for future expansion.

Is SoFi Stock a Good Buy?

Analyst sentiment toward SoFi Technologies (SOFI) is rated as a Hold, based on the consensus of 16 Wall Street analysts tracked in the last three months. Of these ratings, four analysts call it a Buy, eight recommend a Hold, and four recommend a Sell.

The average 12-month SOFI price target sits at $26.96. This target implies a slight downside potential of 0.74% from the last price.
data.author: Annika Masrani
